title: First rocket launch!
description: Print and launch your own model rocket. Follow the steps to print, assemble, and launch the rocket. Get ready for liftoff!

dialogue.text: Nova here. First launch is my favorite. We'll print, assemble, and fly safely. Ready to light the fuse?

options.text: You betcha! How, uh, how high is this rocket thing launching?

dialogue.text: I've flown this design before. Expect ~35 meters in calm air; wind decides the rest.

options.text: Oh, cool, that doesn't sound very high, but I'm sure I'm underestimating what that looks like! And I don't need some special paperwork from the government to launch this thing?

dialogue.text: FAA hobby limits are friendly: under 1500 g total and under 125 g propellant. We're around 90 g body and 18 g motor. Find a wide, open launch site.

options.text: Gotcha, that quiets my fears a bit. So, what do I need to do to get this thing ready to launch?

dialogue.text: First step: 3D print the rocket in lightweight PLA. It's about an hour on a tuned printer.

options.text: Print the rocket?? Aren't there, like, pre-made rockets I can buy?

dialogue.text: Sure, you can buy one. But building teaches you why it flies. We'll print four parts, glue, and get airborne. MVP now, upgrades later.

options.text: Works for me! What do I need to print?

dialogue.text: Modular parts keep failures small and upgrades easy. Print a nosecone, body tube, fincan, and nosecone coupler. One printer means four runs; take your time.

options.text: Fin! Well, not yet, but soon!
A couple of these should do the trick! Get it? A couple? Nosecone coupler? Hah! Actually I only need the one ...
Alright, all 4 components are now printed! What's next?

dialogue.text: Assembly time. You'll need a solid motor, superglue, and Kevlar shock cord. Grab them, then follow the schematic to glue and rig the recovery line.

options.text: The rocket's assembled! Are we ready for launch?

dialogue.text: Almost there. You'll need a launch pad, igniter, and launch controller. The pad keeps it straight; the controller lights it. Here—spares from my kit.

options.text: Oh cool, I'll never turn down free stuff!
We are go for launch!!! Right?

dialogue.text: Place the rocket on the pad, insert the igniter, connect the controller, and step back. On your call, press launch and track the flight.

options.text: Whoa! I can't believe I launched a rocket!! It didn't survive the landing, though, unfortunately.

dialogue.text: Still a huge win. You're on your way to rocketeer status. We'll iterate the design and aim for a clean recovery next time.

options.text: Houston, we do not have a problem!
